decks.name: Noses (Poem)
decks.description: Learn about the fun poem that celebrates different kinds of noses

cards.back: The poem 'Noses' is about the many different types of noses that people and animals have. It celebrates how every nose is unique and special in its own way.
hints: Think about the title of the poem

cards.front: According to the poem, what are some shapes noses can be?
cards.back: Noses can be long, short, turned-up, flat, pointed, snub, or big. The poem tells us noses come in all shapes and sizes!
hints: Think about how noses look different

cards.front: Why does the poet say noses are important?
cards.back: The poet says noses are important because they sit right in the middle of our face and help us smell things. Everyone needs a nose!
hints: Where is a nose on your face?

cards.front: What is the main message of the poem 'Noses'?
cards.back: The main message is that all noses are wonderful, no matter what shape or size they are. We should appreciate our own nose and not wish for someone else's.

cards.front: What does 'snub nose' mean in the poem?
cards.back: A 'snub nose' is a short, slightly flat nose that turns up a little at the tip. It is one of the many types of noses the poet describes.
hints: It is a type of short nose

decks.name: The Little Fir Tree (Story)
decks.description: Learn about the story of the fir tree that wished for different leaves

cards.front: Why was the little fir tree unhappy?
cards.back: The little fir tree was unhappy because it had needle-like leaves instead of broad, pretty leaves like other trees. It wished it had different leaves.

cards.front: What was the fir tree's first wish and what happened?
cards.back: The fir tree first wished for leaves of gold. It got golden leaves, but a man came and stole all the gold leaves, leaving the tree bare.

cards.front: What was the fir tree's second wish and what happened?
cards.back: The fir tree then wished for glass leaves. It got beautiful glass leaves, but a strong wind came and broke all the glass leaves into pieces.

cards.front: What was the fir tree's third wish and what happened?
cards.back: The fir tree wished for broad green leaves like other trees. It got them, but a goat came along and ate up all the green leaves.

cards.front: What did the fir tree learn at the end of the story?
cards.back: The fir tree learned that its own needle-like leaves were the best for it. No one could steal them, break them, or eat them. The story teaches us to be happy with what we have.
hints: It wanted its own leaves back

decks.name: Grammar: Nouns
decks.description: Learn about naming words from the grammar section of Unit 2

cards.back: A noun is a naming word. It is the name of a person, place, animal, or thing. Examples: tree, nose, man, goat, forest, gold.

cards.front: Find the nouns: 'The little fir tree stood in the forest.'
cards.back: 'Tree' and 'forest' are the nouns. 'Tree' names a thing and 'forest' names a place.

cards.front: What is the difference between a common noun and a proper noun?
cards.back: A common noun is a general name like 'tree', 'boy', or 'city'. A proper noun is a specific name like 'India', 'Riya', or 'Ganga'. Proper nouns always start with a capital letter.
hints: One is general, one is specific

cards.front: List all the nouns in: 'A goat ate the green leaves of the tree.'
cards.back: The nouns are 'goat', 'leaves', and 'tree'. 'Goat' names an animal, 'leaves' and 'tree' name things.

cards.front: Can you give examples of nouns from the poem 'Noses'?
cards.back: Nouns from the poem include: nose, face, people, animals, tip, shape. All of these are naming words for people, body parts, or things.
hints: Think of naming words in the poem
